---
title: "Research Agenda"
description-meta: "Theoretical map and editorial agenda for computational social research."
page-navigation: true
toc: false
page-layout: full
format:
  html:
    title-block: false
---

```{=html}
<div class="agenda-shell">
  <section class="agenda-page-hero" data-lang="en">
    <div class="page-eyebrow">Research Agenda</div>
    <h1 class="page-title" style="color:#ffffff; border-bottom:none; padding-bottom:0; margin-bottom:0.75rem;">Conceptual network and research pipeline</h1>
    <p class="page-subtitle" style="color:rgba(255,255,255,0.68); max-width:760px;">
      A live map of the ideas, methods, and publication pathways that currently organize my work in computational social science.
    </p>
  </section>

  <section class="agenda-page-hero" data-lang="es" style="display:none;">
    <div class="page-eyebrow">Agenda de investigacion</div>
    <h1 class="page-title" style="color:#ffffff; border-bottom:none; padding-bottom:0; margin-bottom:0.75rem;">Red conceptual y pipeline de investigacion</h1>
    <p class="page-subtitle" style="color:rgba(255,255,255,0.68); max-width:760px;">
      Un mapa vivo de ideas, metodos y posibles salidas editoriales que hoy organizan mi trabajo en ciencia social computacional.
    </p>
  </section>

  <section class="page-wrap" data-lang="en">
    <div class="page-hero">
      <div class="page-eyebrow">Theoretical Approach</div>
      <h2 class="page-title">Dynamic conceptual network</h2>
      <p class="page-subtitle">This is the live version of the map. Nodes and links can be edited directly in <code>agenda.qmd</code> through the <code>NODES</code> and <code>EDGES</code> arrays below.</p>
    </div>
    <div class="page-content">
      <div class="agenda-network-wrap">
        <div class="network-canvas" id="theoretical-network">
          <canvas aria-label="Interactive theoretical network"></canvas>
          <div class="network-tooltip" id="network-tooltip"></div>
        </div>
        <div class="network-legend">
          <span><i style="background:#8b7cf8;"></i>Computational</span>
          <span><i style="background:#fb923c;"></i>Methods</span>
          <span><i style="background:#2dd4bf;"></i>Political</span>
          <span><i style="background:#f472b6;"></i>Social</span>
        </div>
      </div>
      <p class="network-note">Keep only this live plot: add or remove concepts by editing <code>NODES</code> and <code>EDGES</code> in <code>agenda.qmd</code>.</p>
    </div>
  </section>

  <section class="page-wrap" data-lang="es" style="display:none;">
    <div class="page-hero">
      <div class="page-eyebrow">Aproximacion teorica</div>
      <h2 class="page-title">Red conceptual dinamica</h2>
      <p class="page-subtitle">Esta es la version viva del mapa. Los nodos y enlaces se editan directamente en <code>agenda.qmd</code>, dentro de los arreglos <code>NODES</code> y <code>EDGES</code>.</p>
    </div>
    <div class="page-content">
      <div class="agenda-network-wrap">
        <div class="network-canvas" id="theoretical-network-es">
          <canvas aria-label="Red teorica interactiva"></canvas>
          <div class="network-tooltip" id="network-tooltip-es"></div>
        </div>
        <div class="network-legend">
          <span><i style="background:#8b7cf8;"></i>Computacional</span>
          <span><i style="background:#fb923c;"></i>Metodos</span>
          <span><i style="background:#2dd4bf;"></i>Politico</span>
          <span><i style="background:#f472b6;"></i>Social</span>
        </div>
      </div>
      <p class="network-note">Nos quedamos solo con este plot vivo: agrega o elimina conceptos editando <code>NODES</code> y <code>EDGES</code> en <code>agenda.qmd</code>.</p>
    </div>
  </section>

  <section class="page-wrap agenda-entry-module" data-lang="en">
    <div class="page-hero">
      <div class="page-eyebrow">Research Pipeline</div>
      <h2 class="page-title">Literature review and smart agenda</h2>
      <p class="page-subtitle">Each entry now tracks a clear research question, the empirical or conceptual material behind it, and the most plausible output.</p>
    </div>
    <div class="page-content">
      <p class="agenda-entry-intro">Think of this section as a working intelligence board: not a blog archive, but a compact log of what is being clarified, with what materials, and toward which deliverable.</p>
      <div class="agenda-filter-row" role="toolbar" aria-label="Filter agenda entries">
        <button class="agenda-filter-btn is-active" type="button" data-filter="all">All</button>
        <button class="agenda-filter-btn" type="button" data-filter="literature">Literature Review</button>
        <button class="agenda-filter-btn" type="button" data-filter="methods">Methods Note</button>
        <button class="agenda-filter-btn" type="button" data-filter="post">Upcoming Post</button>
      </div>
      <div class="agenda-entry-list">
        <article class="agenda-entry" data-entry-type="literature">
          <div class="agenda-entry-meta">
            <span class="agenda-badge agenda-badge--lit">Literature Review</span>
            <span class="agenda-date">April 2026</span>
          </div>
          <h3>How is democratic legitimacy narrated in digital public arenas?</h3>
          <p>A review focused on how legitimacy, state authority, and contestation are framed when political discourse moves across newspapers, platform traces, and institutional crises.</p>
          <div class="agenda-intel-grid">
            <div class="agenda-intel-item">
              <span class="agenda-intel-label">Question</span>
              <p>Which narrative patterns connect discourse, legitimacy, and democratic erosion?</p>
            </div>
            <div class="agenda-intel-item">
              <span class="agenda-intel-label">Material</span>
              <p>Press archives, legitimacy literature, radical-right discourse, and comparative work on democratic trust.</p>
            </div>
            <div class="agenda-intel-item">
              <span class="agenda-intel-label">Output</span>
              <p>Conceptual memo for a paper introduction and a sharper literature review section.</p>
            </div>
          </div>
          <div class="agenda-tags">
            <span class="agenda-tag">NLP</span>
            <span class="agenda-tag">Legitimacy</span>
            <span class="agenda-tag">Media archives</span>
          </div>
        </article>

        <article class="agenda-entry" data-entry-type="methods">
          <div class="agenda-entry-meta">
            <span class="agenda-badge agenda-badge--methods">Methods Note</span>
            <span class="agenda-date">May 2026</span>
          </div>
          <h3>What makes a newspaper corpus analytically trustworthy over time?</h3>
          <p>A methods note on the practical problem behind longitudinal press datasets: scraping consistency, metadata quality, version control, and the transition from raw text to reusable research objects.</p>
          <div class="agenda-intel-grid">
            <div class="agenda-intel-item">
              <span class="agenda-intel-label">Question</span>
              <p>How should a longitudinal text pipeline be documented so later inference remains auditable?</p>
            </div>
            <div class="agenda-intel-item">
              <span class="agenda-intel-label">Material</span>
              <p>Scraping scripts, corpus metadata, validation checks, and reproducible workflows in R.</p>
            </div>
            <div class="agenda-intel-item">
              <span class="agenda-intel-label">Output</span>
              <p>Short methods note for MethodoLab plus a reusable checklist for future corpus projects.</p>
            </div>
          </div>
          <div class="agenda-tags">
            <span class="agenda-tag">Web scraping</span>
            <span class="agenda-tag">R workflows</span>
            <span class="agenda-tag">Reproducibility</span>
          </div>
        </article>

        <article class="agenda-entry" data-entry-type="post">
          <div class="agenda-entry-meta">
            <span class="agenda-badge agenda-badge--post">Upcoming Post</span>
            <span class="agenda-date">June 2026</span>
          </div>
          <h3>What exactly is lost when a panel loses respondents across waves?</h3>
          <p>An editorial note designed to translate attrition into a substantive issue: not only a technical problem, but a challenge for inference, comparison, and interpretation in intercultural panel work.</p>
          <div class="agenda-intel-grid">
            <div class="agenda-intel-item">
              <span class="agenda-intel-label">Question</span>
              <p>When does attrition alter the story we think the ELRI panel is telling?</p>
            </div>
            <div class="agenda-intel-item">
              <span class="agenda-intel-label">Material</span>
              <p>Wave-to-wave continuity, nonresponse patterns, and substantive shifts in intercultural attitudes.</p>
            </div>
            <div class="agenda-intel-item">
              <span class="agenda-intel-label">Output</span>
              <p>Public-facing post that explains panel logic without flattening the methodological stakes.</p>
            </div>
          </div>
          <div class="agenda-tags">
            <span class="agenda-tag">Survey methods</span>
            <span class="agenda-tag">ELRI</span>
            <span class="agenda-tag">Panels</span>
          </div>
        </article>

        <article class="agenda-entry" data-entry-type="post">
          <div class="agenda-entry-meta">
            <span class="agenda-badge agenda-badge--post">Upcoming Post</span>
            <span class="agenda-date">July 2026</span>
          </div>
          <h3>When does an agent-based model actually clarify a political mechanism?</h3>
          <p>A short note aimed at separating fashionable simulation from explanatory usefulness, especially in discussions of polarization, trust breakdown, and democratic instability.</p>
          <div class="agenda-intel-grid">
            <div class="agenda-intel-item">
              <span class="agenda-intel-label">Question</span>
              <p>Which democratic processes are illuminated by simulation, and which are only redescribed?</p>
            </div>
            <div class="agenda-intel-item">
              <span class="agenda-intel-label">Material</span>
              <p>ABM literature, polarization research, and conceptual debates on mechanism-based explanation.</p>
            </div>
            <div class="agenda-intel-item">
              <span class="agenda-intel-label">Output</span>
              <p>Explainer post connecting theory, simulation, and empirical relevance for social scientists.</p>
            </div>
          </div>
          <div class="agenda-tags">
            <span class="agenda-tag">ABM</span>
            <span class="agenda-tag">Democracy</span>
            <span class="agenda-tag">Polarization</span>
          </div>
        </article>
      </div>
    </div>
  </section>

  <section class="page-wrap agenda-entry-module" data-lang="es" style="display:none;">
    <div class="page-hero">
      <div class="page-eyebrow">Pipeline de investigacion</div>
      <h2 class="page-title">Revision de literatura y agenda inteligente</h2>
      <p class="page-subtitle">Cada entrada ahora deja explicita una pregunta de investigacion, el material empírico o conceptual que la sostiene y la salida mas probable.</p>
    </div>
    <div class="page-content">
      <p class="agenda-entry-intro">Piensa esta seccion como un tablero de trabajo analitico: no como archivo de posts, sino como un registro compacto de que se esta aclarando, con que material, y hacia que entregable podria avanzar.</p>
      <div class="agenda-filter-row" role="toolbar" aria-label="Filtrar entradas">
        <button class="agenda-filter-btn is-active" type="button" data-filter="all">Todo</button>
        <button class="agenda-filter-btn" type="button" data-filter="literature">Literature Review</button>
        <button class="agenda-filter-btn" type="button" data-filter="methods">Methods Note</button>
        <button class="agenda-filter-btn" type="button" data-filter="post">Upcoming Post</button>
      </div>
      <div class="agenda-entry-list">
        <article class="agenda-entry" data-entry-type="literature">
          <div class="agenda-entry-meta">
            <span class="agenda-badge agenda-badge--lit">Literature Review</span>
            <span class="agenda-date">Abril 2026</span>
          </div>
          <h3>Como se narra la legitimidad democratica en las arenas publicas digitales?</h3>
          <p>Una revision centrada en como se encuadran legitimidad, autoridad estatal y disputa politica cuando el discurso circula entre prensa digital, trazas de plataformas y crisis institucionales.</p>
          <div class="agenda-intel-grid">
            <div class="agenda-intel-item">
              <span class="agenda-intel-label">Pregunta</span>
              <p>Que patrones narrativos conectan discurso, legitimidad y erosion democratica?</p>
            </div>
            <div class="agenda-intel-item">
              <span class="agenda-intel-label">Material</span>
              <p>Archivos de prensa, literatura sobre legitimidad, discurso de derecha radical y confianza democratica.</p>
            </div>
            <div class="agenda-intel-item">
              <span class="agenda-intel-label">Salida</span>
              <p>Memo conceptual para una introduccion de paper y una revision bibliografica mas precisa.</p>
            </div>
          </div>
          <div class="agenda-tags">
            <span class="agenda-tag">NLP</span>
            <span class="agenda-tag">Legitimidad</span>
            <span class="agenda-tag">Archivos de prensa</span>
          </div>
        </article>

        <article class="agenda-entry" data-entry-type="methods">
          <div class="agenda-entry-meta">
            <span class="agenda-badge agenda-badge--methods">Methods Note</span>
            <span class="agenda-date">Mayo 2026</span>
          </div>
          <h3>Que vuelve confiable a un corpus de prensa cuando se analiza en el tiempo?</h3>
          <p>Una nota metodologica sobre el problema practico detras de los datasets longitudinales de prensa: consistencia del scraping, calidad de metadatos, versionado y paso de texto crudo a objeto reutilizable de investigacion.</p>
          <div class="agenda-intel-grid">
            <div class="agenda-intel-item">
              <span class="agenda-intel-label">Pregunta</span>
              <p>Como debe documentarse un pipeline longitudinal de texto para que la inferencia posterior siga siendo auditable?</p>
            </div>
            <div class="agenda-intel-item">
              <span class="agenda-intel-label">Material</span>
              <p>Scripts de scraping, metadatos del corpus, validaciones y flujos reproducibles en R.</p>
            </div>
            <div class="agenda-intel-item">
              <span class="agenda-intel-label">Salida</span>
              <p>Nota corta para MethodoLab y checklist reutilizable para futuros proyectos de corpus.</p>
            </div>
          </div>
          <div class="agenda-tags">
            <span class="agenda-tag">Web scraping</span>
            <span class="agenda-tag">Flujos en R</span>
            <span class="agenda-tag">Reproducibilidad</span>
          </div>
        </article>

        <article class="agenda-entry" data-entry-type="post">
          <div class="agenda-entry-meta">
            <span class="agenda-badge agenda-badge--post">Upcoming Post</span>
            <span class="agenda-date">Junio 2026</span>
          </div>
          <h3>Que se pierde exactamente cuando un panel pierde casos entre olas?</h3>
          <p>Una nota editorial pensada para traducir la attricion en un problema sustantivo: no solo tecnico, sino interpretativo para la comparacion y la inferencia en trabajo panel intercultural.</p>
          <div class="agenda-intel-grid">
            <div class="agenda-intel-item">
              <span class="agenda-intel-label">Pregunta</span>
              <p>En que momento la attricion cambia la historia que creemos que cuenta el panel ELRI?</p>
            </div>
            <div class="agenda-intel-item">
              <span class="agenda-intel-label">Material</span>
              <p>Continuidad entre olas, patrones de no respuesta y cambios en actitudes interculturales.</p>
            </div>
            <div class="agenda-intel-item">
              <span class="agenda-intel-label">Salida</span>
              <p>Post publico que explique la logica panel sin simplificar demasiado las implicancias metodologicas.</p>
            </div>
          </div>
          <div class="agenda-tags">
            <span class="agenda-tag">Encuestas</span>
            <span class="agenda-tag">ELRI</span>
            <span class="agenda-tag">Paneles</span>
          </div>
        </article>

        <article class="agenda-entry" data-entry-type="post">
          <div class="agenda-entry-meta">
            <span class="agenda-badge agenda-badge--post">Upcoming Post</span>
            <span class="agenda-date">Julio 2026</span>
          </div>
          <h3>Cuando un modelo basado en agentes realmente aclara un mecanismo politico?</h3>
          <p>Una nota breve orientada a separar simulacion de moda de utilidad explicativa, especialmente en discusiones sobre polarizacion, quiebre de confianza e inestabilidad democratica.</p>
          <div class="agenda-intel-grid">
            <div class="agenda-intel-item">
              <span class="agenda-intel-label">Pregunta</span>
              <p>Que procesos democraticos ilumina la simulacion y cuales solo vuelve a describir?</p>
            </div>
            <div class="agenda-intel-item">
              <span class="agenda-intel-label">Material</span>
              <p>Literatura ABM, investigacion sobre polarizacion y debates sobre explicacion basada en mecanismos.</p>
            </div>
            <div class="agenda-intel-item">
              <span class="agenda-intel-label">Salida</span>
              <p>Post explicativo que conecte teoria, simulacion y relevancia empirica para ciencias sociales.</p>
            </div>
          </div>
          <div class="agenda-tags">
            <span class="agenda-tag">ABM</span>
            <span class="agenda-tag">Democracia</span>
            <span class="agenda-tag">Polarizacion</span>
          </div>
        </article>
      </div>
    </div>
  </section>
</div>
```

```{=html}
<script>
(function () {
  // <- EDIT HERE TO ADD NODES
  const NODES = [
    { id: "nlp", label: "NLP", color: "#8b7cf8", size: 34, cat: "comp", desc: "Natural Language Processing - discourse analysis at scale" },
    { id: "abm", label: "ABM", color: "#8b7cf8", size: 26, cat: "comp", desc: "Agent-Based Modelling - emergent social behavior" },
    { id: "trace", label: "Digital Trace", color: "#fb923c", size: 24, cat: "meth", desc: "Behavioral footprints on platforms and news sites" },
    { id: "demo", label: "Democracy", color: "#2dd4bf", size: 30, cat: "pol", desc: "Democratic erosion and legitimacy in Latin America" },
    { id: "ai", label: "AI", color: "#8b7cf8", size: 28, cat: "comp", desc: "LLMs, embeddings, classification for social research" },
    { id: "violence", label: "Violence", color: "#f472b6", size: 22, cat: "soc", desc: "Social legitimacy of violence - EPSEP study" },
    { id: "inter", label: "Intercultural", color: "#f472b6", size: 22, cat: "soc", desc: "Indigenous relations in Chile - ELRI study" },
    { id: "scraping", label: "Web Scraping", color: "#fb923c", size: 20, cat: "meth", desc: "Automated extraction of newspaper archives" },
    { id: "survey", label: "Survey Methods", color: "#fb923c", size: 20, cat: "meth", desc: "Longitudinal design, panel attrition, multilevel models" },
    { id: "polar", label: "Polarization", color: "#2dd4bf", size: 18, cat: "pol", desc: "Affective and ideological polarization via text + survey" }
  ];

  const EDGES = [
    ["nlp", "abm"], ["nlp", "ai"], ["nlp", "polar"], ["nlp", "trace"], ["nlp", "scraping"],
    ["abm", "demo"], ["abm", "violence"], ["abm", "polar"],
    ["trace", "scraping"], ["trace", "ai"], ["trace", "polar"],
    ["demo", "violence"], ["demo", "inter"], ["demo", "polar"],
    ["ai", "scraping"], ["ai", "polar"], ["violence", "inter"],
    ["survey", "nlp"], ["survey", "abm"]
  ];

  function bootNetwork(containerId, tooltipId) {
    const container = document.getElementById(containerId);
    if (!container) return;

    const canvas = container.querySelector("canvas");
    const tooltip = document.getElementById(tooltipId);
    const ctx = canvas.getContext("2d");
    const dpr = window.devicePixelRatio || 1;
    const nodes = NODES.map(function (node, index) {
      return Object.assign({}, node, {
        x: 120 + (index % 5) * 120,
        y: 90 + Math.floor(index / 5) * 110,
        vx: 0,
        vy: 0
      });
    });
    const lookup = new Map(nodes.map(function (node) { return [node.id, node]; }));
    const edges = EDGES.map(function (pair) {
      return { source: lookup.get(pair[0]), target: lookup.get(pair[1]) };
    }).filter(function (edge) {
      return edge.source && edge.target;
    });

    let width = 0;
    let height = 0;
    let hovered = null;
    let dragged = null;
    let pointer = { x: 0, y: 0 };
    let tick = 0;

    function resize() {
      width = container.clientWidth;
      height = container.clientHeight;
      canvas.width = width * dpr;
      canvas.height = height * dpr;
      canvas.style.width = width + "px";
      canvas.style.height = height + "px";
      ctx.setTransform(dpr, 0, 0, dpr, 0, 0);
    }

    function clamp(node) {
      const radius = node.size * 0.7;
      node.x = Math.max(radius, Math.min(width - radius, node.x));
      node.y = Math.max(radius, Math.min(height - radius, node.y));
    }

    function applyPhysics() {
      for (let i = 0; i < nodes.length; i += 1) {
        for (let j = i + 1; j < nodes.length; j += 1) {
          const a = nodes[i];
          const b = nodes[j];
          let dx = b.x - a.x;
          let dy = b.y - a.y;
          const distSq = Math.max(dx * dx + dy * dy, 36);
          const force = 1800 / distSq;
          const dist = Math.sqrt(distSq);
          dx /= dist;
          dy /= dist;
          a.vx -= dx * force;
          a.vy -= dy * force;
          b.vx += dx * force;
          b.vy += dy * force;
        }
      }

      edges.forEach(function (edge) {
        const dx = edge.target.x - edge.source.x;
        const dy = edge.target.y - edge.source.y;
        const dist = Math.max(Math.sqrt(dx * dx + dy * dy), 1);
        const rest = 120;
        const spring = (dist - rest) * 0.0025;
        const ux = dx / dist;
        const uy = dy / dist;
        edge.source.vx += ux * spring;
        edge.source.vy += uy * spring;
        edge.target.vx -= ux * spring;
        edge.target.vy -= uy * spring;
      });

      nodes.forEach(function (node) {
        if (dragged === node) {
          node.x = pointer.x;
          node.y = pointer.y;
          node.vx = 0;
          node.vy = 0;
        } else {
          const pull = 0.0008;
          node.vx += (width / 2 - node.x) * pull;
          node.vy += (height / 2 - node.y) * pull;
          node.vx *= 0.96;
          node.vy *= 0.96;
          node.x += node.vx;
          node.y += node.vy;
        }
        clamp(node);
      });
    }

    function drawParticles(edge, progress) {
      const x = edge.source.x + (edge.target.x - edge.source.x) * progress;
      const y = edge.source.y + (edge.target.y - edge.source.y) * progress;
      ctx.beginPath();
      ctx.fillStyle = "rgba(255,255,255,0.65)";
      ctx.arc(x, y, 2.1, 0, Math.PI * 2);
      ctx.fill();
    }

    function draw() {
      ctx.clearRect(0, 0, width, height);

      edges.forEach(function (edge, index) {
        ctx.beginPath();
        ctx.strokeStyle = hovered && (hovered === edge.source || hovered === edge.target) ? "rgba(255,255,255,0.45)" : "rgba(88,77,134,0.65)";
        ctx.lineWidth = hovered && (hovered === edge.source || hovered === edge.target) ? 1.4 : 0.9;
        ctx.moveTo(edge.source.x, edge.source.y);
        ctx.lineTo(edge.target.x, edge.target.y);
        ctx.stroke();

        drawParticles(edge, ((tick * 0.007) + index * 0.15) % 1);
      });

      nodes.forEach(function (node) {
        const pulse = Math.sin(tick * 0.04 + node.size) * 1.4;
        const radius = node.size * 0.62 + pulse;
        const isHot = hovered === node;

        ctx.beginPath();
        ctx.fillStyle = node.color;
        ctx.shadowColor = isHot ? node.color : "rgba(0,0,0,0)";
        ctx.shadowBlur = isHot ? 24 : 0;
        ctx.arc(node.x, node.y, radius, 0, Math.PI * 2);
        ctx.fill();

        ctx.beginPath();
        ctx.fillStyle = "rgba(255,255,255,0.92)";
        ctx.font = "500 12px Inter, sans-serif";
        ctx.textAlign = "center";
        ctx.textBaseline = "middle";
        ctx.fillText(node.label, node.x, node.y);
      });
      ctx.shadowBlur = 0;
    }

    function render() {
      tick += 1;
      applyPhysics();
      draw();
      requestAnimationFrame(render);
    }

    function setTooltip(node) {
      if (!tooltip) return;
      if (!node) {
        tooltip.classList.remove("is-visible");
        return;
      }
      tooltip.innerHTML = "<h4>" + node.label + "</h4><p>" + node.desc + "</p>";
      tooltip.style.left = Math.min(node.x + 18, width - 220) + "px";
      tooltip.style.top = Math.max(node.y - 10, 12) + "px";
      tooltip.classList.add("is-visible");
    }

    function hitTest(x, y) {
      return nodes.find(function (node) {
        const dx = x - node.x;
        const dy = y - node.y;
        return Math.sqrt(dx * dx + dy * dy) < node.size * 0.68;
      }) || null;
    }

    function pointerPos(evt) {
      const rect = canvas.getBoundingClientRect();
      return {
        x: evt.clientX - rect.left,
        y: evt.clientY - rect.top
      };
    }

    canvas.addEventListener("mousemove", function (evt) {
      pointer = pointerPos(evt);
      hovered = hitTest(pointer.x, pointer.y);
      setTooltip(hovered);
      canvas.style.cursor = hovered ? "grab" : "default";
    });

    canvas.addEventListener("mousedown", function (evt) {
      pointer = pointerPos(evt);
      dragged = hitTest(pointer.x, pointer.y);
      if (dragged) {
        canvas.style.cursor = "grabbing";
      }
    });

    window.addEventListener("mouseup", function () {
      dragged = null;
      canvas.style.cursor = hovered ? "grab" : "default";
    });

    canvas.addEventListener("mouseleave", function () {
      hovered = null;
      dragged = null;
      setTooltip(null);
      canvas.style.cursor = "default";
    });

    window.addEventListener("resize", resize);
    resize();
    render();
  }

  function wireAgendaFilters() {
    document.querySelectorAll(".agenda-entry-module").forEach(function (module) {
      const buttons = module.querySelectorAll(".agenda-filter-btn");
      const entries = module.querySelectorAll(".agenda-entry");
      buttons.forEach(function (button) {
        button.addEventListener("click", function () {
          const filter = button.getAttribute("data-filter");
          buttons.forEach(function (candidate) {
            candidate.classList.toggle("is-active", candidate === button);
          });
          entries.forEach(function (entry) {
            const show = filter === "all" || entry.getAttribute("data-entry-type") === filter;
            entry.style.display = show ? "" : "none";
          });
        });
      });
    });
  }

  document.addEventListener("DOMContentLoaded", function () {
    bootNetwork("theoretical-network", "network-tooltip");
    bootNetwork("theoretical-network-es", "network-tooltip-es");
    wireAgendaFilters();
  });
})();
</script>
```
